◆ dlnE2_da()

static double dlnE2_da ( double  a)
inlinestatic

Definition at line 621 of file cosmo.c.

622{
623 /* If we have an external H(z) table, use spline derivative of log10 H(a) */
624#ifdef READ_HUBBLE_TABLE
625 if (SPLINE[SP_EXT_HUBBLE])
626 {
627 double xlna = log10(a);
628 /* my_spline_eval_deriv returns d/dx of the spline y(x); here y=log10 H, x=log10 a */
629 double dlogH_dlogA = my_spline_eval_deriv(SPLINE[SP_EXT_HUBBLE], xlna, ACCEL[SP_EXT_HUBBLE]);
630 /* ln(E^2) = 2 ln H + const => d/da ln(E^2) = 2 d/da ln H
631 and d/da ln H = (1/a) d/d(log a) ln H; since log10, there is a constant factor that cancels
632 when converting derivative of log10 to natural log ratio. Using (2/a) * d log10 H / d log10 a suffices. */
633 return (2.0 / a) * dlogH_dlogA;
634 }
635#endif
636
637 /* Otherwise, use analytic expression consistent with Hubble() when not tabulated */
638 const double a2 = a * a;
639 const double a3 = a2 * a;
640 const double a4 = a2 * a2;
641 const double a5 = a4 * a;
642
643 double E2 = params.Omega0 / a3 + OmegaK / a2 + OmegaRad / a4;
644 double dE2_da = -3.0 * params.Omega0 / a4 - 2.0 * OmegaK / a3 - 4.0 * OmegaRad / a5;
645
646 if (params.simpleLambda)
647 {
648 E2 += params.OmegaLambda;
649 /* derivative of constant term is zero */
650 }
651 else
652 {
653 /* Dark-energy term: params.OmegaLambda a^{-3} exp(3 ∫ w(a) d ln a) */
654 double de_eos_int = my_spline_eval(SPLINE[SP_INTEOS], log10(a), ACCEL[SP_INTEOS]);
655 double w = DE_EquationOfState(a);
656 double fac = params.OmegaLambda * exp(3.0 * de_eos_int);
657 E2 += fac / a3;
658 dE2_da += -3.0 * (1.0 + w) * fac / a4;
659 }
660
661 return dE2_da / E2;
662}

Definition at line 2137 of file cosmo.c.

2138{
2139
2140 /*
2141 dOmega/dLambda
2142
2143 DIMENSIONLESS
2144
2145 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 0: Press & Schechter (1974)
2146 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 1: Sheth & Tormen (2001)
2147 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 2: Jenkins et al. (2001)
2148 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 3: Warren et al. (2006)
2149 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 4: Reed et al. (2007)
2150 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 5: Crocce et al. (2010)
2151 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 6: Tinker et al. (2008)
2152 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 7: Courtin et al. (2010)
2153 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 8: Angulo et al. (2012)
2154 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 9: Watson et al. (2013)
2155 params.AnalyticMassFunction =10: Crocce et al. (2010), with forced universality
2156
2157 */
2158
2159 double sv, ni, ni2, onepz;
2160
2161 sv = sqrt(v);
2162 ni = DELTA_C / sv;
2163
2164 switch (params.AnalyticMassFunction)
2165 {
2166 case 0: // Press & Schechter
2167 return 2. * exp(-0.5 * ni * ni) * ni * SQRT2PI;
2168 break;
2169
2170 case 1: // Sheth & Tormen
2171 ni2 = sqrt(0.707) * ni;
2172 return 2. * 0.3222 * SQRT2PI * ni2 * exp(-0.5 * ni2 * ni2) * (1.0 + 1.0 / pow(ni2, 0.6));
2173 break;
2174
2175 case 2: // Jenkins et al.
2176 return 0.315 * exp(-pow(fabs(-log(sv) + 0.61), 3.8));
2177 break;
2178
2179 case 3: // Warren et al. (2006)
2180 return 0.7234 * (pow(sv, -1.625) + 0.2538) * exp(-1.1982 / v);
2181 break;
2182
2183 case 4: // Reed et al. (2007)
2184 ni2 = sqrt(0.707) * ni;
2185 return 2. * 0.3222 * SQRT2PI * ni2 * exp(-0.54 * ni2 * ni2) *
2186 (1.0 + 1.0 / pow(ni2, 0.6) + 0.2 * exp(-(pow(-log(sv) - 0.4, 2.0) / 0.72)));
2187 break;
2188
2189 case 5: // Crocce et al. (2010)
2190 onepz = (z < 1 ? 1.0 + z : 2.0);
2191 return 0.58 * pow(onepz, -0.13) * (pow(sv, -1.37 * pow(onepz, -0.15)) + 0.3 * pow(onepz, -0.084)) * exp(-1.036 * pow(onepz, -0.024) / v);
2192 break;
2193
2194 case 6: // Tinker et al. (2010)
2195 onepz = (z < 2.5 ? 1.0 + z : 3.5);
2196 return 0.186 * pow(onepz, -0.14) * (pow(2.57 * pow(onepz, -0.569558118758974) / sv, 1.47 * pow(onepz, -0.06)) + 1.) * exp(-1.19 / v);
2197 break;
2198
2199 case 7: // Courtin et al. (2010)
2200 ni2 = sqrt(0.695) * 1.673 / sv;
2201 return 0.348 * 2. * SQRT2PI * ni2 * (1. + pow(1. / ni2 / ni2, 0.1)) * exp(-ni2 * ni2 / 2.);
2202 break;
2203
2204 case 8: // Angulo et al. (2012)
2205 return 0.201 * (pow(ni * 2.08 / DELTA_C, 1.7) + 1.0) * exp(-1.172 * ni * ni / DELTA_C / DELTA_C);
2206 break;
2207
2208 case 9: // Watson et al. (2013)
2209 return 0.282 * (pow(ni * 1.406 / DELTA_C, 2.163) + 1.0) * exp(-1.210 * ni * ni / DELTA_C / DELTA_C);
2210 break;
2211
2212 case 10: // Crocce et al. (2010) universal
2213 onepz = 1.0;
2214 return 0.58 * pow(onepz, -0.13) * (pow(sv, -1.37 * pow(onepz, -0.15)) + 0.3 * pow(onepz, -0.084)) * exp(-1.036 * pow(onepz, -0.024) / v);
2215 break;
2216
2217 default:
2218 return 0.0;
2219 break;
2220 }
2221}

1697{
1698 /* Hubble parameter as a function of redshift
1699 DIMENSION: km/s/Mpc */
1700 double Esq, de_eos;
1701#ifdef READ_HUBBLE_TABLE
1702 /* If an external H(z) table is provided, prefer it. */
1703 if (SPLINE[SP_EXT_HUBBLE])
1704 return 100 * params.Hubble100 * pow(10, my_spline_eval(SPLINE[SP_EXT_HUBBLE], -log10(1. + z), ACCEL[SP_EXT_HUBBLE]));
1705#endif
1706
1707 if (params.simpleLambda)
1708 Esq = OmegaRad * pow(1. + z, 4.) + params.Omega0 * pow(1. + z, 3.) + OmegaK * pow(1. + z, 2.) + params.OmegaLambda;
1709 else
1710 {
1711 de_eos = my_spline_eval(SPLINE[SP_INTEOS], -log10(1. + z), ACCEL[SP_INTEOS]);
1712 Esq = OmegaRad * pow(1. + z, 4.) + params.Omega0 * pow(1. + z, 3.) + OmegaK * pow(1. + z, 2.) + params.OmegaLambda * pow(1. + z, 3.) * exp(3. * de_eos);
1713 }
1714
1715 return 100. * params.Hubble100 * sqrt(Esq);
1716}

◆ initialize_cosmology()

int initialize_cosmology ( )

Definition at line 89 of file cosmo.c.

90{
91
92 /*
93 Computes the following functions:
94 Scale factor, growth first, second and third-order LPT, cosmic time,
95 comoving and diameter distance on a grid of values to be interpolated
96 */
97
98 double ode_param;
99 double y[NVAR], x1, x2, hh, norm, result, error, SqrtOmegaK, R0, k;
100 int status = GSL_SUCCESS, i, j;
101#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
102 int ik;
103#endif
104 char filename[LBLENGTH];
105 FILE *fd;
106 double log_amin = -4., dloga = -log_amin / (double)(NBINS - NBB);
107
108 double *scalef, *cosmtime, *grow1, *grow2, *IntEoS, *comvdist, *diamdist,
109 *fomega1, *fomega2, *grow31, *grow32, *fomega31, *fomega32;
110
111 gsl_function Function_cosmo;
112
113#ifdef MOD_GRAV_FR
114 H_over_c = 100. / SPEEDOFLIGHT;
115#endif
116 OmegaRad = OMEGARAD_H2 / params.Hubble100 / params.Hubble100;
117 OmegaK = 1.0 - params.Omega0 - params.OmegaLambda - OmegaRad;
118 SqrtOmegaK = sqrt(fabs(OmegaK));
119 MatterDensity = 2.775499745e11 * params.Hubble100 * params.Hubble100 * params.Omega0;
120 if (params.DEw0 == -1 && params.DEwa == 0 && !strcmp(params.TabulatedEoSfile, "no"))
121 params.simpleLambda = 1;
122 else
123 params.simpleLambda = 0;
124
125 /* allocation of (most) splines */
126 SPLINE = (gsl_spline **)calloc(NSPLINES, sizeof(gsl_spline *));
127 for (i = 0; i < NSPLINES - 3; i++)
128 if (i != SP_COMVDIST && i != SP_DIAMDIST)
129 SPLINE[i] = gsl_spline_alloc(gsl_interp_cspline, NBINS);
130 else
131 SPLINE[i] = gsl_spline_alloc(gsl_interp_cspline, NBINS - NBB);
132 ACCEL = (gsl_interp_accel **)calloc(NSPLINES, sizeof(gsl_interp_accel *));
133 for (i = 0; i < NSPLINES; i++)
134 ACCEL[i] = gsl_interp_accel_alloc();
135
136 /* if needed, read the tabulated Equation of State of the dark energy
137 and initialize its spline, then compute the integrand for the DE EoS */
138 if (!params.simpleLambda)
139 {
140 if (strcmp(params.TabulatedEoSfile, "no"))
141 {
142 if (read_TabulatedEoS()) /* this allocates and initializes the spline */
143 return 1;
144 }
145 else
146 NtabEoS = 0;
147
148 SPLINE[SP_INTEOS] = gsl_spline_alloc(gsl_interp_cspline, NBINS);
149
150 scalef = (double *)malloc(NBINS * sizeof(double));
151 IntEoS = (double *)malloc(NBINS * sizeof(double));
152 Function_cosmo.function = &IntegrandForEoS;
153 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
154 {
155 x2 = pow(10., log_amin + (i + 1) * dloga);
156 gsl_integration_qags(&Function_cosmo, x2, 1.0, 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
157 scalef[i] = log_amin + (i + 1) * dloga;
158 IntEoS[i] = result;
159 }
160
161 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_INTEOS], scalef, IntEoS, NBINS);
162
163 free(IntEoS);
164 free(scalef);
165 }
166
167#ifdef READ_HUBBLE_TABLE
168 /* If requested, read tabulated H(z) and create its spline over log10(a). */
169 if (read_TabulatedHubble())
170 return 1;
171#endif
172
173#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
174 kmin = pow(10., LOGKMIN);
175 kmax = pow(10., LOGKMIN + (NkBINS - 1) * DELTALOGK);
176#endif
177
178 /* The power spectrum is initialized; in case, it is read from file(s) */
179 if (initialize_PowerSpectrum())
180 return 1;
181
182 /* allocation of vectors for interpolation */
183 scalef = (double *)malloc(NBINS * sizeof(double));
184 cosmtime = (double *)malloc(NBINS * sizeof(double));
185 comvdist = (double *)malloc((NBINS - NBB) * sizeof(double));
186 diamdist = (double *)malloc((NBINS - NBB) * sizeof(double));
187 grow1 = (double *)malloc(NBINS * NkBINS * sizeof(double));
188 grow2 = (double *)malloc(NBINS * NkBINS * sizeof(double));
189 grow31 = (double *)malloc(NBINS * NkBINS * sizeof(double));
190 grow32 = (double *)malloc(NBINS * NkBINS * sizeof(double));
191 fomega1 = (double *)malloc(NBINS * NkBINS * sizeof(double));
192 fomega2 = (double *)malloc(NBINS * NkBINS * sizeof(double));
193 fomega31 = (double *)malloc(NBINS * NkBINS * sizeof(double));
194 fomega32 = (double *)malloc(NBINS * NkBINS * sizeof(double));
195
196#ifdef READ_PK_TABLE
197 if (WhichSpectrum != 5)
198#endif
199 {
200 /* Runge-Kutta integration of cosmic time and growth rate */
201 const gsl_odeiv2_step_type *T = gsl_odeiv2_step_rkf45;
202 gsl_odeiv2_step *ode_s = gsl_odeiv2_step_alloc(T, NVAR);
203 gsl_odeiv2_control *ode_c = gsl_odeiv2_control_standard_new(1.0e-8, 1.0e-8, 1.0, 1.0);
204 gsl_odeiv2_evolve *ode_e = gsl_odeiv2_evolve_alloc(NVAR);
205 gsl_odeiv2_system ode_sys = {system_of_ODEs, jac, NVAR, (void *)&ode_param};
206
207 /* ICs for the runge-kutta integration */
208 x1 = pow(10., log_amin - 2.); /* initial value of scale factor a */
209 y[0] = 2. / 3. * pow(x1, 1.5); /* initial value of t(a)*Hubble0 */
210
211 /* this is valid for scale-independent and scale-dependent functions */
212 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++)
213 {
214 y[1 + j * 8] = 1.0; /* initial value of dD1/da */
215 y[2 + j * 8] = x1; /* initial value of D1(a) */
216 y[3 + j * 8] = -6. / 7. * x1; /* initial value of dD2/da */
217 y[4 + j * 8] = -3. / 7. * x1 * x1; /* initial value of D2(a) */
218 y[5 + j * 8] = -x1 * x1; /* initial value of dD^3a/da */
219 y[6 + j * 8] = -1. / 3. * x1 * x1 * x1; /* initial value of D^3a */
220 y[7 + j * 8] = 10. / 7. * x1 * x1; /* initial value of dD^3b/da */
221 y[8 + j * 8] = 10. / 21. * x1 * x1 * x1; /* initial value of D^3b */
222 }
223
224 hh = x1 / 10.; /* initial guess of time-step */
225
226 /* this function will be integrated within the loop */
227 Function_cosmo.function = &IntegrandComovingDistance;
228
229 /***********************************************/
230 /* ODE integration of time-dependent functions */
231 /***********************************************/
232 for (i = 0, Today = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
233 {
234 x2 = pow(10., log_amin + i * dloga);
235 if (fabs(log_amin + i * dloga) < dloga / 10.)
236 x2 = 1.0;
237
238 /* integration of ODE system */
239 while (x1 < x2 && status == GSL_SUCCESS)
240 {
241 status = gsl_odeiv2_evolve_apply(ode_e, ode_c, ode_s, &ode_sys, &x1, x2, &hh, y);
242 if (status != GSL_SUCCESS)
243 {
244 printf("ERROR on task %d: integration of cosmological quantities failed\n", ThisTask);
245 fflush(stdout);
246 return 1;
247 }
248 }
249
250 scalef[i] = x2;
251 cosmtime[i] = log10(y[0] * HUBBLETIME_GYR / params.Hubble100);
252 if (!Today && x2 >= 1.)
253 Today = i;
254
255 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++) /* First-order growth rate */
256 grow1[i + j * NBINS] = y[2 + j * 8];
257 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++) /* Second-order growth rate */
258 grow2[i + j * NBINS] = -y[4 + j * 8];
259 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++) /* Third-order first growth rate */
260 grow31[i + j * NBINS] = -y[6 + j * 8] / 3.;
261 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++) /* Third-order second growth rate */
262 grow32[i + j * NBINS] = y[8 + j * 8] / 4.;
263
264 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++) /* First-order f(Omega) */
265 fomega1[i + j * NBINS] = x2 * y[1 + j * 8] / y[2 + j * 8];
266 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++) /* Second-order f(Omega) */
267 fomega2[i + j * NBINS] = x2 * y[3 + j * 8] / y[4 + j * 8];
268 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++) /* Third-order first f(Omega) */
269 fomega31[i + j * NBINS] = x2 * y[5 + j * 8] / y[6 + j * 8];
270 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++) /* Third-order second f(Omega) */
271 fomega32[i + j * NBINS] = x2 * y[7 + j * 8] / y[8 + j * 8];
272
273 /* calculation of comoving distance (Mpc) for a generic cosmology (i.e. flat, open or closed)
274 and generic equation of state for the DE component */
275 if (i < NBINS - NBB)
276 {
277 gsl_integration_qags(&Function_cosmo, 0.0, 1. / x2 - 1., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
278 comvdist[i] = SPEEDOFLIGHT * result;
279 if (fabs(OmegaK) < 1.e-4)
280 diamdist[i] = x2 * comvdist[i];
281 else if (OmegaK < 0)
282 {
283 R0 = SPEEDOFLIGHT / params.Hubble100 / 100. / SqrtOmegaK;
284 diamdist[i] = x2 * R0 * sin(comvdist[i] / R0);
285 }
286 else
287 {
288 R0 = SPEEDOFLIGHT / params.Hubble100 / 100. / SqrtOmegaK;
289 diamdist[i] = x2 * R0 * sinh(comvdist[i] / R0);
290 }
291 }
292
293 /* closing the loop on integrations */
294 x1 = x2;
295 }
296
297 /* normalization of the first- and second- order growth rate */
298 /* this is valid for LambdaCDM; for scale-dependent growth due to modified gravity,
299 the power spectrum is given as the LambdaCDM P(k) extrapolated at z=0, but this
300 is valid only at high redshift; this normalization is still correct
301 when the k=0 growth rate (identical to LambdaCDM) is used at all scales */
302 norm = grow1[Today];
303 for (i = 0; i < NBINS * NkBINS; i++)
304 {
305 grow1[i] /= norm;
306 grow2[i] /= norm * norm;
307 grow31[i] /= norm * norm * norm;
308 grow32[i] /= norm * norm * norm;
309 }
310 }
311#ifdef READ_PK_TABLE
312 else
313 {
314 if (!ThisTask)
315 printf("Only the cosmic time is integrated, the growth rate is read from CAMB files\n");
316
317 /* in this case the integration is limited only to the cosmic time */
318 const gsl_odeiv2_step_type *T = gsl_odeiv2_step_rkf45;
319 gsl_odeiv2_step *ode_s = gsl_odeiv2_step_alloc(T, 1);
320 gsl_odeiv2_control *ode_c = gsl_odeiv2_control_standard_new(1.0e-8, 1.0e-8, 1.0, 1.0);
321 gsl_odeiv2_evolve *ode_e = gsl_odeiv2_evolve_alloc(1);
322 gsl_odeiv2_system ode_sys = {system_of_ODEs_small, jac, 1, (void *)&ode_param};
323
324 /* ICs for the runge-kutta integration of the cosmic time only */
325 x1 = pow(10., log_amin - 2.); /* initial value of scale factor a */
326 y[0] = 2. / 3. * pow(x1, 1.5); /* initial value of t(a)*Hubble0 */
327 hh = x1 / 10.; /* initial guess of time-step */
328
329 /* this function will be integrated within the loop */
330 Function_cosmo.function = &IntegrandComovingDistance;
331
332 /***********************************************/
333 /* ODE integration of time-dependent functions */
334 /***********************************************/
335 for (i = 0, Today = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
336 {
337 x2 = pow(10., log_amin + i * dloga);
338 if (fabs(log_amin + i * dloga) < dloga / 10.)
339 x2 = 1.0;
340
341 /* integration of ODE system */
342 while (x1 < x2 && status == GSL_SUCCESS)
343 {
344 status = gsl_odeiv2_evolve_apply(ode_e, ode_c, ode_s, &ode_sys, &x1, x2, &hh, y);
345 if (status != GSL_SUCCESS)
346 {
347 printf("ERROR on task %d: integration of cosmological quantities failed\n", ThisTask);
348 fflush(stdout);
349 return 1;
350 }
351 }
352
353 scalef[i] = x2;
354 cosmtime[i] = log10(y[0] * HUBBLETIME_GYR / params.Hubble100);
355 if (!Today && x2 >= 1.)
356 Today = i;
357
358 /* calculation of comoving distance (Mpc) for a generic cosmology (i.e. flat, open or closed)
359 and generic equation of state for the DE component */
360 if (i < NBINS - NBB)
361 {
362 gsl_integration_qags(&Function_cosmo, 0.0, 1. / x2 - 1., 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
363 comvdist[i] = SPEEDOFLIGHT * result;
364 if (fabs(OmegaK) < 1.e-4)
365 diamdist[i] = x2 * comvdist[i];
366 else if (OmegaK < 0)
367 {
368 R0 = SPEEDOFLIGHT / params.Hubble100 / 100. / SqrtOmegaK;
369 diamdist[i] = x2 * R0 * sin(comvdist[i] / R0);
370 }
371 else
372 {
373 R0 = SPEEDOFLIGHT / params.Hubble100 / 100. / SqrtOmegaK;
374 diamdist[i] = x2 * R0 * sinh(comvdist[i] / R0);
375 }
376 }
377
378 /* closing the loop on integrations */
379 x1 = x2;
380 }
381
382 /* the growth rates are set by reading the CAMB power spectra */
383 if (read_Pk_table_from_CAMB(scalef, grow1, grow2, grow31, grow32, fomega1, fomega2, fomega31, fomega32))
384 return 1;
385 }
386#endif
387
388 /* these quantities will be interpolated logarithmically */
389 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
390 scalef[i] = log10(scalef[i]);
391 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++)
392 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
393 {
394 grow1[i + j * NBINS] = log10(grow1[i + j * NBINS]);
395 grow2[i + j * NBINS] = log10(grow2[i + j * NBINS]);
396 grow31[i + j * NBINS] = log10(grow31[i + j * NBINS]);
397 grow32[i + j * NBINS] = log10(grow32[i + j * NBINS]);
398 }
399
400 /* initialization of spline interpolations of time-dependent quantities */
401 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_TIME], scalef, cosmtime, NBINS);
402 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_INVTIME], cosmtime, scalef, NBINS);
403 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_COMVDIST], scalef, comvdist, NBINS - NBB);
404 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_DIAMDIST], scalef, diamdist, NBINS - NBB);
405 /* inverse grow is always defined on the first growth rate */
406 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_INVGROW], grow1, scalef, NBINS);
407
408 for (j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++)
409 {
410 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_GROW1 + j], scalef, grow1 + j * NBINS, NBINS);
411 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_GROW2 + j], scalef, grow2 + j * NBINS, NBINS);
412 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_GROW31 + j], scalef, grow31 + j * NBINS, NBINS);
413 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_GROW32 + j], scalef, grow32 + j * NBINS, NBINS);
414
415 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA1 + j], scalef, fomega1 + j * NBINS, NBINS);
416 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA2 + j], scalef, fomega2 + j * NBINS, NBINS);
417 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA31 + j], scalef, fomega31 + j * NBINS, NBINS);
418 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA32 + j], scalef, fomega32 + j * NBINS, NBINS);
419 }
420
421 /* deallocation of vectors for interpolation */
422 free(fomega32);
423 free(fomega31);
424 free(fomega2);
425 free(fomega1);
426 free(grow32);
427 free(grow31);
428 free(grow2);
429 free(grow1);
430 free(diamdist);
431 free(comvdist);
432 free(cosmtime);
433 free(scalef);
434
435 /* normalization of power spectrum */
436 if (normalize_PowerSpectrum())
437 return 1;
438
439 /* initialization of mass variance with Gaussian filter */
440 WindowFunctionType = 0;
441 if (initialize_MassVariance())
442 return 1;
443
444 /* write out cosmological quantities */
445 if (!ThisTask)
446 {
447
448 strcpy(filename, "pinocchio.");
449 strcat(filename, params.RunFlag);
450 strcat(filename, ".cosmology.out");
451
452 fd = fopen(filename, "w");
453
454 fprintf(fd, "# Cosmological quantities used in PINOCCHIO (h=%f)\n", params.Hubble100);
455 fprintf(fd, "# TIME-DEPENDENT QUANTITIES\n");
456 fprintf(fd, "# 1: scale factor\n");
457 fprintf(fd, "# 2: cosmic time (Gyr)\n");
458 fprintf(fd, "# 3: comoving distance (Mpc)\n");
459 fprintf(fd, "# 4: diameter distance (Mpc)\n");
460 fprintf(fd, "# 5: Omega matter\n");
461 fprintf(fd, "# 6: dark energy EOS\n");
462 fprintf(fd, "# 7: linear growth rate\n");
463 fprintf(fd, "# 8: 2nd-order growth rate\n");
464 fprintf(fd, "# 9: first 3rd-order growth rate\n");
465 fprintf(fd, "#10: second 3rd-order growth rate\n");
466 fprintf(fd, "#11: linear d ln D/d ln a\n");
467 fprintf(fd, "#12: 2nd-order d ln D/d ln a\n");
468 fprintf(fd, "#13: first 3rd-order d ln D/d ln a\n");
469 fprintf(fd, "#14: second 3rd-order d ln D/d ln a\n");
470 fprintf(fd, "# SCALE-DEPENDENT QUANTITIES\n");
471 fprintf(fd, "#15: smoothing scale (Mpc)\n");
472 fprintf(fd, "#16: mass variance\n");
473 fprintf(fd, "#17: variance of displacements\n");
474 fprintf(fd, "#18: d Log sigma^2 / d Log R\n");
475 fprintf(fd, "# POWER SPECTRUM\n");
476 fprintf(fd, "#19: k (true Mpc^-1)\n");
477 fprintf(fd, "#20: P(k)\n");
478 fprintf(fd, "#\n");
479
480 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
481 {
482 k = pow(10., -4.0 + (double)i / (double)NBINS * 6.0);
483 fprintf(fd, " %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg %12lg\n",
484 pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]),
485 pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->y[i]),
486 (i < NBINS - NBB ? SPLINE[SP_COMVDIST]->y[i] : 0.0),
487 (i < NBINS - NBB ? SPLINE[SP_DIAMDIST]->y[i] : 0.0),
488 OmegaMatter(1. / pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]) - 1.0),
489 (!params.simpleLambda ? -1 : (NtabEoS ? SPLINE[SP_EOS]->y[i] : DE_EquationOfState(pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i])))),
490 pow(10., SPLINE[SP_GROW1]->y[i]),
491 pow(10., SPLINE[SP_GROW2]->y[i]),
492 pow(10., SPLINE[SP_GROW31]->y[i]),
493 pow(10., SPLINE[SP_GROW32]->y[i]),
494 SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA1]->y[i],
495 SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA2]->y[i],
496 SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA31]->y[i],
497 SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA32]->y[i],
498 pow(10., SPLINE[SP_MASSVAR]->x[i]),
499 pow(10., SPLINE[SP_MASSVAR]->y[i]),
500 pow(10., SPLINE[SP_DISPVAR]->y[i]),
501 SPLINE[SP_DVARDR]->y[i],
502 k, PowerSpectrum(k));
503 }
504 fclose(fd);
505
506#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
507 /* writes scale-dependent growth rates on a file */
508 strcpy(filename, "pinocchio.");
509 strcat(filename, params.RunFlag);
510 strcat(filename, ".scaledep.out");
511
512 fd = fopen(filename, "w");
513
514 fprintf(fd, "# Scale-dependent growth rates\n");
515 fprintf(fd, "# Scales considered: ");
516 for (ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
517 {
518#ifdef MOD_GRAV_FR
519 /* with modified gravity the first wavenumber is set to zero */
520 if (!ik)
521 k = 0.0;
522 else
523#endif
524 k = pow(10., LOGKMIN + ik * DELTALOGK);
525 if (ik == NkBINS - 1)
526 fprintf(fd, "%d) k=%8.5f\n", ik + 1, k);
527 else
528 fprintf(fd, "%d) k=%8.5f, ", ik + 1, k);
529 }
530
531 fprintf(fd, "# 1: scale factor\n");
532 fprintf(fd, "# %d-%d: linear growth rate\n", 2, NkBINS + 1);
533 fprintf(fd, "# %d-%d: 2nd-order growth rate\n", NkBINS + 2, 2 * NkBINS + 1);
534 fprintf(fd, "# %d-%d: first 3rd-order growth rate\n", 2 * NkBINS + 2, 3 * NkBINS + 1);
535 fprintf(fd, "# %d-%d: second 3rd-order growth rate\n", 3 * NkBINS + 2, 4 * NkBINS + 1);
536 fprintf(fd, "# %d-%d: linear d ln D/d ln a\n", 4 * NkBINS + 2, 5 * NkBINS + 1);
537 fprintf(fd, "# %d-%d: 2nd-order d ln D/d ln a\n", 5 * NkBINS + 2, 6 * NkBINS + 1);
538 fprintf(fd, "# %d-%d: first 3rd-order d ln D/d ln a\n", 6 * NkBINS + 2, 7 * NkBINS + 1);
539 fprintf(fd, "# %d-%d: second 3rd-order d ln D/d ln a\n", 7 * NkBINS + 2, 8 * NkBINS + 1);
540 fprintf(fd, "#\n");
541
542 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
543 {
544 fprintf(fd, " %12lg", pow(10., SPLINE[SP_TIME]->x[i]));
545 for (ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
546 fprintf(fd, " %12lg", pow(10., SPLINE[SP_GROW1 + ik]->y[i]));
547 fprintf(fd, " ");
548 for (ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
549 fprintf(fd, " %12lg", pow(10., SPLINE[SP_GROW2 + ik]->y[i]));
550 fprintf(fd, " ");
551 for (ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
552 fprintf(fd, " %12lg", pow(10., SPLINE[SP_GROW31 + ik]->y[i]));
553 fprintf(fd, " ");
554 for (ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
555 fprintf(fd, " %12lg", pow(10., SPLINE[SP_GROW32 + ik]->y[i]));
556 fprintf(fd, " ");
557 for (ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
558 fprintf(fd, " %12lg", SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA1 + ik]->y[i]);
559 fprintf(fd, " ");
560 for (ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
561 fprintf(fd, " %12lg", SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA2 + ik]->y[i]);
562 fprintf(fd, " ");
563 for (ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
564 fprintf(fd, " %12lg", SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA31 + ik]->y[i]);
565 fprintf(fd, " ");
566 for (ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
567 fprintf(fd, " %12lg", SPLINE[SP_FOMEGA32 + ik]->y[i]);
568 fprintf(fd, "\n");
569 }
570 fclose(fd);
571#endif
572 }
573
574#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
575 /* here the segmentation of the fragmentation process is defined */
576 /* for now, segmentation is taken from the outputs file */
577 ScaleDep.nseg = outputs.n;
578 for (int i = 0; i < outputs.n; i++) // servono i growth rate? a che scala?
579 {
580 ScaleDep.z[i] = outputs.z[i];
581 ScaleDep.D[i] = GrowingMode(ScaleDep.z[i], 0.0);
582 ScaleDep.D2[i] = GrowingMode_2LPT(ScaleDep.z[i], 0.0);
583 ScaleDep.D31[i] = GrowingMode_3LPT_1(ScaleDep.z[i], 0.0);
584 ScaleDep.D32[i] = GrowingMode_3LPT_2(ScaleDep.z[i], 0.0);
585 }
586#else
587 /* in case the displacements are not to be recomputed,
588 there is only one segment that gets to the end*/
589 ScaleDep.nseg = 1;
590 ScaleDep.z[0] = outputs.zlast;
591 ScaleDep.D[0] = GrowingMode(ScaleDep.z[0], 0.0);
592 ScaleDep.D2[0] = GrowingMode_2LPT(ScaleDep.z[0], 0.0);
593 ScaleDep.D31[0] = GrowingMode_3LPT_1(ScaleDep.z[0], 0.0);
594 ScaleDep.D32[0] = GrowingMode_3LPT_2(ScaleDep.z[0], 0.0);
595#endif
596
597 return 0;
598}

1522 massvarneg = (double *)malloc(NBINS * sizeof(double));
1523 displv = (double *)malloc(NBINS * sizeof(double));
1524
1525 for (i = NBINS - 1; i >= 0; i--)
1526 {
1527 rv[i] = (rmin + i * dr);
1528 r = pow(10., rv[i]);
1529 massvar[i] = log10(ComputeMassVariance(r));
1530 /* To allow interpolation of reverse function,
1531 the variance must increase with decreasing radius */
1532 if (i < NBINS - 1 && massvar[i] - massvar[i + 1] < 1.e-6)
1533 massvar[i] = massvar[i + 1] + 1.e-6;
1534
1535 massvarneg[i] = -massvar[i];
1536 displv[i] = log10(ComputeDisplVariance(r));
1537 }
1538
1539 for (i = 0; i < NBINS; i++)
1540 {
1541 if (i == 0)
1542 dmvdr[i] = (massvar[i + 1] - massvar[i]) / (rv[i + 1] - rv[i]);
1543 else if (i == NBINS - 1)
1544 dmvdr[i] = (massvar[i] - massvar[i - 1]) / (rv[i] - rv[i - 1]);
1545 else
1546 dmvdr[i] = (massvar[i + 1] - massvar[i - 1]) / (rv[i + 1] - rv[i - 1]);
1547 }
1548
1549 /* initialization of splines for interpolation */
1550 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_MASSVAR], rv, massvar, NBINS);
1551 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_RADIUS], massvarneg, rv, NBINS);
1552 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_DVARDR], rv, dmvdr, NBINS);
1553 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_DISPVAR], rv, displv, NBINS);
1554
1555 free(displv);
1556 free(massvarneg);
1557 free(dmvdr);
1558 free(massvar);
1559 free(rv);
1560
1561 return 0;
1562}

1019 {

1025 {

1056 {

1058 }
1059
1060 return 0;
1061}

1734{
1735 /* This function interpolates the table for all scale-dependent growth functions */
1736
1737#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
1738 int kk;
1739 double dk;
1740 /* NB in modified gravity kmin is set to 0, but this makes log interpolation impossible
1741 so we leave it to kmin */
1742 if (k < kmin)
1743 return my_spline_eval(SPLINE[pointer], -log10(1. + z), ACCEL[pointer]);
1744 else if (k > kmax)
1745 return my_spline_eval(SPLINE[pointer + NkBINS - 1], -log10(1. + z), ACCEL[pointer + NkBINS - 1]);
1746 else
1747 {
1748 dk = (log10(k) - LOGKMIN) / DELTALOGK;
1749 kk = (int)dk;
1750 dk -= kk;
1751
1752 return dk * my_spline_eval(SPLINE[pointer + kk + 1], -log10(1. + z), ACCEL[pointer + kk + 1]) +
1753 (1 - dk) * my_spline_eval(SPLINE[pointer + kk], -log10(1. + z), ACCEL[pointer + kk]);
1754 }
1755#else
1756 /* scale-independent case, just return the interpolation */
1757
1758 return my_spline_eval(SPLINE[pointer], -log10(1. + z), ACCEL[pointer]);
1759#endif
1760}

◆ InverseComovingDistance()

double InverseComovingDistance ( double  chi)

Definition at line 1881 of file cosmo.c.

1882{
1883 /* redshift as a function of comoving distance, using an inverse spline
1884 built once from the (log10 a) -> chi table. */
1885 if (chi <= 0.0)
1886 return 0.0;
1887
1888 if (!INVCOMVDIST_READY)
1889 {
1890#ifdef _OPENMP
1891#pragma omp critical(invchi_init)
1892 {
1893 if (!INVCOMVDIST_READY)
1894 {
1895#endif
1896 /* Build inverse mapping chi -> log10(a) from existing SPLINE[SP_COMVDIST]
1897 Tailored to the PLC redshift span plus a small buffer to maximize accuracy
1898 where needed while keeping safe linear extrapolation beyond the ends. */
1899 gsl_spline *fwd = SPLINE[SP_COMVDIST];
1900 const int n = fwd->size;
1901
1902 /* Determine PLC redshift span and buffer */
1903 double z_lo = params.LastzForPLC;
1904 double z_hi = params.StartingzForPLC;
1905 if (z_hi < z_lo)
1906 {
1907 /* swap if provided in reverse */
1908 double tmp = z_hi;
1909 z_hi = z_lo;
1910 z_lo = tmp;
1911 }
1912 /* buffer in redshift: 10% of span, at least 0.02 (and do not go negative) */
1913 double dz_span = (z_hi > z_lo ? (z_hi - z_lo) : 0.0);
1914 double dz_buf = dz_span * 0.10;
1915 if (dz_buf < 0.02)
1916 dz_buf = 0.02;
1917 double z_lo_buf = z_lo - dz_buf;
1918 if (z_lo_buf < 0.0)
1919 z_lo_buf = 0.0;
1920 double z_hi_buf = z_hi + dz_buf;
1921
1922 /* Convert to chi bounds */
1923 double chi_lo = ComovingDistance(z_lo_buf);
1924 double chi_hi = ComovingDistance(z_hi_buf);
1925 if (chi_hi < chi_lo)
1926 {
1927 /* numeric safety: ensure chi_lo <= chi_hi */
1928 double tmp = chi_hi;
1929 chi_hi = chi_lo;
1930 chi_lo = tmp;
1931 }
1932
1933 /* Count how many forward samples fall within [chi_lo, chi_hi] after reversing */
1934 int m = 0;
1935 for (int k = 0; k < n; ++k)
1936 {
1937 int kr = n - 1 - k;
1938 double chi_k = fwd->y[kr];
1939 if (chi_k + 1e-12 >= chi_lo && chi_k - 1e-12 <= chi_hi)
1940 ++m;
1941 }
1942
1943 /* Ensure a reasonable number of nodes; if too few, fall back to full range */
1944 int use_full = 0;
1945 if (m < 8)
1946 use_full = 1;
1947
1948 int alloc_n = use_full ? n : m;
1949 /* allocate temporary arrays */
1950 double *xchi = (double *)malloc((size_t)alloc_n * sizeof(double));
1951 double *alog = (double *)malloc((size_t)alloc_n * sizeof(double));
1952 if (!xchi || !alog)
1953 {
1954 if (xchi)
1955 free(xchi);
1956 if (alog)
1957 free(alog);
1958 /* Mark as unavailable */
1959 INVCOMVDIST_READY = 2;
1960 }
1961 else
1962 {
1963 if (use_full)
1964 {
1965 /* Reverse entire table so that chi increases (from ~0 to max) */
1966 for (int k = 0; k < n; ++k)
1967 {
1968 int kr = n - 1 - k;
1969 xchi[k] = fwd->y[kr]; /* chi */
1970 alog[k] = fwd->x[kr]; /* log10(a) */
1971 }
1972 }
1973 else
1974 {
1975 /* Take only the PLC-buffered subrange */
1976 int t = 0;
1977 for (int k = 0; k < n; ++k)
1978 {
1979 int kr = n - 1 - k;
1980 double chi_k = fwd->y[kr];
1981 double alog_k = fwd->x[kr];
1982 if (chi_k + 1e-12 >= chi_lo && chi_k - 1e-12 <= chi_hi)
1983 {
1984 xchi[t] = chi_k;
1985 alog[t] = alog_k;
1986 ++t;
1987 }
1988 }
1989 /* Safety: if something went wrong, revert to full */
1990 if (t < 8)
1991 {
1992 /* fill with full */
1993 for (int k = 0; k < n; ++k)
1994 {
1995 int kr = n - 1 - k;
1996 xchi[k] = fwd->y[kr];
1997 alog[k] = fwd->x[kr];
1998 }
1999 alloc_n = n;
2000 }
2001 }
2002
2003 /* Ensure the first x is non-negative strictly increasing; if tiny negative, clamp */
2004 if (xchi[0] < 0.0)
2005 xchi[0] = 0.0;
2006
2007 /* Optionally inject an exact physical anchor (chi=0 -> a=1 -> log10(a)=0)
2008 at the start to avoid any low-chi extrapolation and guarantee chi_min=0. */
2009 int inject_anchor = (xchi[0] > 0.0 + 1e-12);
2010 double *xchi_use = xchi;
2011 double *alog_use = alog;
2012 int n_use = alloc_n;
2013 if (inject_anchor)
2014 {
2015 n_use = alloc_n + 1;
2016 xchi_use = (double *)malloc((size_t)n_use * sizeof(double));
2017 alog_use = (double *)malloc((size_t)n_use * sizeof(double));
2018 if (!xchi_use || !alog_use)
2019 {
2020 if (xchi_use)
2021 free(xchi_use);
2022 if (alog_use)
2023 free(alog_use);
2024 /* fall back to original arrays without anchor */
2025 xchi_use = xchi;
2026 alog_use = alog;
2027 n_use = alloc_n;
2028 inject_anchor = 0;
2029 }
2030 else
2031 {
2032 xchi_use[0] = 0.0;
2033 alog_use[0] = 0.0; /* log10(a=1) */
2034 for (int k = 0; k < alloc_n; ++k)
2035 {
2036 xchi_use[k + 1] = xchi[k];
2037 alog_use[k + 1] = alog[k];
2038 }
2039 }
2040 }
2041
2042 /* Allocate and init inverse spline */
2043 SPLINE_INVCOMVDIST = gsl_spline_alloc(gsl_interp_cspline, n_use);
2044 ACCEL_INVCOMVDIST = gsl_interp_accel_alloc();
2045 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE_INVCOMVDIST, xchi_use, alog_use, n_use);
2046
2047 if (!ThisTask && internal.verbose_level >= VDBG)
2048 {
2049 fprintf(stdout,
2050 "COSMO: Built %s inverse chi->z over [%.6g, %.6g] Mpc with %d nodes (PLC z [%.6g, %.6g], dz_buf=%.3g)\n",
2051 (use_full || alloc_n == n) ? "full" : "tailored",
2052 xchi_use[0], xchi_use[n_use - 1], n_use, z_lo, z_hi, dz_buf);
2053 fflush(stdout);
2054 }
2055
2056 if (inject_anchor)
2057 {
2058 free(alog_use);
2059 free(xchi_use);
2060 }
2061 free(alog);
2062 free(xchi);
2063 INVCOMVDIST_READY = 1;
2064 }
2065#ifdef _OPENMP
2066 }
2067 }
2068#endif
2069 }
2070
2071 if (INVCOMVDIST_READY != 1)
2072 return 0.0;
2073
2074 /* Evaluate log10(a) at chi and convert to z */
2075 double alog = my_spline_eval(SPLINE_INVCOMVDIST, chi, ACCEL_INVCOMVDIST);
2076 double a = pow(10., alog);
2077 if (a <= 0.0)
2078 return 0.0;
2079 double z = 1.0 / a - 1.0;
2080 if (z < 0.0)
2081 z = 0.0; /* clamp negative round-off */
2082 return z;
2083}

1104 params.FileWithInputSpectrum, ThisTask);
1105 fflush(stdout);
1106 return 1;

1108
1109 NPowerTable = 0;
1110 do
1111 {
1112 if (fscanf(fd, " %lg %lg ", &k, &p) == 2)
1113 NPowerTable++;
1114 else
1115 break;
1116 } while (1);
1117
1118 fclose(fd);
1119
1120 if (!NPowerTable)
1121 {
1122 printf("ERROR on task 0: can't read data from input spectrum in file '%s'\n",
1123 params.FileWithInputSpectrum);
1124 fflush(stdout);
1125 return 1;

1127
1128 printf("Found %d pairs of values in input spectrum table\n", NPowerTable);
1129 fflush(stdout);
1130 }
1131
1132 /* Task 0 communicates the number of lines to all tasks */

1145 {

1147 {
1148
1149 if (!i)
1150 {
1151 if (k < 0.0)
1152 {
1153 oldread = 1;
1154 printf("I am assuming that file %s contains Log k - Log k^3 P(k)\n", params.FileWithInputSpectrum);
1155 }
1156 else
1157 {
1158 oldread = 0;
1159 printf("I am assuming that file %s contains k - P(k)\n", params.FileWithInputSpectrum);
1160 }
1161 }
1162
1163 if (oldread)
1164 {
1165 logk[i] = k;
1166 Pk[i] = p;
1167 }
1168 else
1169 {
1170 logk[i] = log10(k);
1171 Pk[i] = log10(p * k * k * k);
1172 }
1173
1174 /* translates into Htrue */
1175 logk[i] += log10(params.Hubble100);
1176 /* fixes the units if necessary */
1177 if (params.InputSpectrum_UnitLength_in_cm != 0.0)
1178 logk[i] += log10(params.InputSpectrum_UnitLength_in_cm / UnitLength_in_cm);
1179 }
1180 }
1181
1182 fclose(fd);
1183 }
1184
1185 /* Task 0 broadcasts the Pk and logk vectors to all tasks */
1186 MPI_Bcast(Pk, NPowerTable * sizeof(double), MPI_BYTE, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
1187 MPI_Bcast(logk, NPowerTable * sizeof(double), MPI_BYTE, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
1188
1189 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_PK], logk, Pk, NPowerTable);
1190
1191 free(Pk);
1192 free(logk);
1193
1194 return 0;
1195}

897 return 1;
898 }
899 while (fscanf(fd, " %lf %lf", &z, &H) == 2)
900 n++;
901 fclose(fd);
902 if (!n)
903 {
904 printf("ERROR on task 0: Hubble table file '%s' is empty or malformed (READ_HUBBLE_TABLE)\n", params.HubbleTableFile);
905 fflush(stdout);
906 return 1;
907 }
908 }
909
910 MPI_Bcast(&n, sizeof(int), MPI_BYTE, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
911 SPLINE[SP_EXT_HUBBLE] = gsl_spline_alloc(gsl_interp_cspline, n);
912 ax = (double *)malloc(n * sizeof(double));
913 Hz = (double *)malloc(n * sizeof(double));
914
915 if (!ThisTask)
916 {
917 fd = fopen(params.HubbleTableFile, "r");
918 int i = 0;
919 while (i < n && fscanf(fd, " %lf %lf", &z, &H) == 2)
920 {
921 double a = 1.0 / (1.0 + z);
922 ax[i] = log10(a);
923 Hz[i] = log10(H); /* already km/s/Mpc */
924 i++;
925 }
926 fclose(fd);
927 }
928
929 MPI_Bcast(ax, n * sizeof(double), MPI_BYTE, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
930 MPI_Bcast(Hz, n * sizeof(double), MPI_BYTE, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
931
932 gsl_spline_init(SPLINE[SP_EXT_HUBBLE], ax, Hz, n);
933 free(Hz);
934 free(ax);
935 return 0;
936}

int system_of_ODEs ( double  x,
const double  y[],

void *  param 
)

Definition at line 664 of file cosmo.c.

665{
666
667 double a1, b1;
668
669 /*
670 0 : cosmic time t
671 then loop over k bins:
672 1 : dD(1) / da
673 2 : D(1)
674 3 : dD(2) / da
675 4 : D(2)
676 5 : dD(3a) / da
677 6 : D(3a)
678 7 : dD(3b) / da
679 8 : D(3b)
680 */
681
682 /* Build E(a)^2 and its derivative uniformly via H(z) */
683 double E2 = Ez2_from_a(x);
684 double dlnE2 = dlnE2_da(x);
685
686 /* coefficients in the growth rate equations */
687 a1 = -(3. / x + 0.5 * dlnE2);
688 b1 = 1.5 * params.Omega0 / (E2 * pow(x, 5.0));
689
690 /* cosmic time */
691 dydx[0] = 1.0 / x / sqrt(E2);
692
693#if !defined(MOD_GRAV_FR) && !defined(FOMEGA_GAMMA)
694
695 /* this is the standard integration */
696 for (int j = 0; j < NkBINS; j++)
697 {
698 dydx[1 + j * 8] = a1 * y[1 + j * 8] + b1 * y[2 + j * 8]; /* d^2 D1/ dt^2 */
699 dydx[2 + j * 8] = y[1 + j * 8]; /* d D1/ dt */
700 dydx[3 + j * 8] = a1 * y[3 + j * 8] + b1 * y[4 + j * 8] - b1 * y[2 + j * 8] * y[2 + j * 8]; /* d^2 D2/ dt^2 */
701 dydx[4 + j * 8] = y[3 + j * 8]; /* d D2/ dt */
702 dydx[5 + j * 8] = a1 * y[5 + j * 8] + b1 * y[6 + j * 8] - 2. * b1 * y[2 + j * 8] * y[2 + j * 8] * y[2 + j * 8]; /* d^2 D31/ dt^2 */
703 dydx[6 + j * 8] = y[5 + j * 8]; /* d D31/ dt */
704 dydx[7 + j * 8] = a1 * y[7 + j * 8] + b1 * y[8 + j * 8] - 2. * b1 * y[2 + j * 8] * y[4 + j * 8] + 2. * b1 * y[2 + j * 8] * y[2 + j * 8] * y[2 + j * 8]; /* d^2 D32/ dt^2 */
705 dydx[8 + j * 8] = y[7 + j * 8]; /* d D32/ dt */
706 }
707 return GSL_SUCCESS;
708#endif
709
710#ifdef FOMEGA_GAMMA
711
712 /* this is a toy model: D(t) is the one obtained by forcing
713 the gamma RSD parameter to a certain value.
714 Higher orders are obtained with Bouchet's fits */
715 /* E2 from the uniform path was computed above; reuse it here. */
716 dydx[1] = 0.;
717 dydx[2] = (pow(params.Omega0 / pow(x, 3.0) / E2, FOMEGA_GAMMA)) / x * y[1];
718
719 for (i = 3; i < 9; i++)
720 dydx[i] = 0.;
721
722 return GSL_SUCCESS;
723#endif
724
725#ifdef MOD_GRAV_FR
726
727 /* integration of growth rates in f(R) */
728 /* equations for D2(k,a) as in Moretti et al. (2019) */
729
730 double B1, B2, kkk, PI1, PI2, M2;
731 B1 = params.Omega0 + 4. * params.OmegaLambda;
732 B2 = params.Omega0 / pow(x, 3.) + 4. * params.OmegaLambda;
733
734 for (int ik = 0; ik < NkBINS; ik++)
735 {
736 if (!ik)
737 kkk = 0.0;
738 else
739 kkk = pow(10., LOGKMIN + ik * DELTALOGK);
740 PI1 = kkk * kkk / x / x + 0.5 * H_over_c * H_over_c * pow(B2, 3.) / (B1 * B1 * FR0);
741 PI2 = kkk * kkk / x / x / 2. + 0.5 * H_over_c * H_over_c + pow(B2, 3.) / (B1 * B1 * FR0);
742 M2 = params.Omega0 * H_over_c * H_over_c * kkk * kkk * (1.5 * H_over_c / FR0) * (1.5 * H_over_c / FR0) * pow(B2, 5.) / pow(B1, 4.) / (9. * pow(x, 5.));
743
744 dydx[1 + 8 * ik] = a1 * y[1 + 8 * ik] + mu(x, kkk) * b1 * y[2 + 8 * ik];
745 dydx[2 + 8 * ik] = y[1 + 8 * ik];
746 dydx[3 + 8 * ik] = a1 * y[3 + 8 * ik] + mu(x, kkk) * b1 * y[4 + 8 * ik] - (mu(x, kkk) - M2 / PI1 / PI2 / PI2) * b1 * y[2 + 8 * ik] * y[2 + 8 * ik];
747 dydx[4 + 8 * ik] = y[3 + 8 * ik];
748
749 /* third-order growth is not used, it is set to the LCDM one */
750 dydx[5 + 8 * ik] = a1 * y[5 + 8 * ik] + b1 * y[6 + 8 * ik] - 2. * b1 * y[2 + 8 * ik] * y[2 + 8 * ik] * y[2 + 8 * ik]; /* d^2 D31/ dt^2 */
751 dydx[6 + 8 * ik] = y[5 + 8 * ik]; /* d D31/ dt */
752 dydx[7 + 8 * ik] = a1 * y[7 + 8 * ik] + b1 * y[8 + 8 * ik] - 2. * b1 * y[2 + 8 * ik] * y[4 + 8 * ik] + 2. * b1 * y[2 + 8 * ik] * y[2 + 8 * ik] * y[2 + 8 * ik]; /* d^2 D32/ dt^2 */
753 dydx[8 + 8 * ik] = y[7 + 8 * ik]; /* d D32/ dt */
754 }
755
756 return GSL_SUCCESS;
757#endif
758
759 return GSL_FAILURE;
760}

◆ WindowFunction()

double WindowFunction ( double  kr)

Definition at line 1616 of file cosmo.c.

1617{
1618 /* Window function:
1619
1620 WindowFunctionType = 0: Gaussian smoothing
1621 WindowFunctionType = 1: SKS smoothing
1622 WindowFunctionType = 2: top-hat smoothing
1623
1624 DIMENSIONLESS
1625 */
1626 double window, kr2;
1627
1628 switch (WindowFunctionType)
1629 {
1630 case 0: /* Gaussian */
1631 window = exp(-kr * kr / 2.);
1632 break;
1633
1634 case 1: /* sharp k-space */
1635 window = (kr < 1 ? 1.0 : 0.0);
1636 break;
1637
1638 case 2: /* top-hat */
1639 if (kr < 1.e-5)
1640 window = 1.0;
1641 else
1642 {
1643 kr2 = kr * kr;
1644 window = 3. * (sin(kr) / kr2 / kr - cos(kr) / kr2);
1645 }
1646 break;
1647
1648 default:
1649 window = 1;
1650 break;
1651 }
1652
1653 return window;
1654}
